articles.title: On average, 11 children killed or wounded every day in past week in Lebanon
articles.summary: On average, 11 children killed or wounded every day in past week in Lebanon An average of 11 children have been killed or wounded every 24 hours in Lebanon, as Israel expanded attacks across the country in spite of a ceasefire being in place. Heavy Israeli ⁠strikes hit towns and villages in southern Lebanon overnight on Wednesday and into Thursday, after Israel declared new swathes of Lebanon "a combat zone". On Thursday, an Israeli strike hit a building in the southern suburbs of Beirut.

Video: He worked for 22 years at Arsenal. Then they fired him over Israel
articles.summary: Video: He worked for 22 years at Arsenal. Then they fired him over Israel Mark Bonnick worked at Arsenal for 22 years. Then on Christmas Eve 2024, the former kitman was fired over posts he made on X criticising Israel over the genocide in Gaza. Arsenal accused him of bringing the club into disrepute. The case was quickly framed by media outlets as one of antisemitism. But reports later revealed that the Football Association told Arsenal his posts had not breached any of the governing body's rules. Mark is now suing Arsenal for unfair dismissal. articles.title: Lebanon warns Israeli strikes put heritage sites in 'serious danger'
articles.summary: Lebanon warns Israeli strikes put heritage sites in 'serious danger' Lebanon's culture minister has told AFP that Israeli strikes on the country's south are putting heritage sites, including in the ancient city of Tyre, in "serious danger". "Bombings fell very close to the ruins of Tyre," a Unesco World Heritage site, Ghassan Salame said, adding that the medieval Beaufort castle overlooking Nabatieh was "directly hit".

articles.title: SpaceX Said to Cut IPO Valuation Goal
articles.summary: SpaceX is currently targeting a valuation of at least $1.8 trillion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg News reported in April that SpaceX was aiming for a valuation above $2 trillion. The target is settling lower after consultations with advisers and investors, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. Matthew Bloxham of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Israel 'added to UN blacklist' for sexual violence in conflict zones Israel  has been added to the United Nations’ blacklist for sexual violence in conflict zones, the country’s ambassador to the UN said on Thursday. Danny Danon  condemned  the decision as “political” and “detached from the facts and from reality”. The move follows multiple reports by human rights organisations and media outlets - including Middle East Eye - documenting allegations of rape and other forms of sexual violence by Israeli forces against  Palestinians  since October 2023. articles.summary: Israel issues expulsion threats in Lebanon's Nabatieh, strikes area in Tyre The Israeli army targeted the town of Maaroub in Tyre, southern Lebanon, the National News Agency reported.  Elsewhere south of the country, the army issued expulsion threats to residents of Ain Qana, in Nabatieh, ordering them to move from the area to a distance of no less than 1000 meters away.

articles.title: Iran's Pezeshkian thanks Malaysia and Pakistan for support
articles.summary: Iran's Pezeshkian thanks Malaysia and Pakistan for support Following conversation with the prime ministers of Malaysia and Pakistan, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has thanked the two countries for their cooperation with Tehran.  "I thanked Malaysia for its humanitarian stance and Pakistan for its initiative and effective efforts to reach an agreement," Pezeshkian said in a post on X. "Iran's policy is to expand cooperation with Muslim and neighbouring countries in all fields," he added.

articles.title: Report: US military planes at Ben Gurion Airport cost Israel $248m in losses
articles.summary: Report: US military planes at Ben Gurion Airport cost Israel $248m in losses The presence of US military planes at Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport has cost airport authorities 700 million shekels ($248m) in losses over the past two months, public broadcaster KAN reported. Quoting Sharon Kedmi, director general of the Israel Airports Authority, the report indicates that the airport is operating at just one-third of its capacity due to the presence of US military refueling aircrafts.
